policies.title: No Surprises - Unexpected features must be opt-in and disclosed
policies.fix: Disclose unexpected behaviors in the listing and require an explicit opt-in inside the add-on UI.

policies.title: Content - Mozilla Acceptable Use Policy
policies.fix: Comply with Mozilla's Acceptable Use Policy.

policies.title: Content - Listings must disclose required payment
policies.fix: If functionality requires payment, disclose this in the AMO listing.

policies.title: Content - Must conform to US laws
policies.fix: Add-on and content must conform to US laws (and may face removal in other jurisdictions where it violates local law).

policies.title: Content - Forks must be clearly distinguished and add value
policies.fix: If your add-on is a fork, name it distinctly and provide significant new functionality.

policies.title: Content - No promo-only / launcher-only add-ons
policies.fix: Add-ons whose sole purpose is to promote, install, load, or launch an outside site/app/add-on are not permitted.

policies.title: Content - Theme image quality requirements
policies.fix: Themes must use high-quality, properly aligned images. Duplicates and stretched/blank images are rejected.

policies.title: Submission - Reviewable source code required
policies.fix: Provide unminified source plus build instructions. Dependencies must come from official package managers.

policies.title: Submission - Functional testing info & credentials
policies.fix: Supply testing instructions and any credentials needed to exercise the add-on.

policies.title: Submission - Corrections must not bundle unrelated changes
policies.fix: When resubmitting after a rejection, only include the requested fixes.

policies.title: Development - Request only necessary permissions
policies.fix: Drop permissions you do not actively use; broad host permissions trigger manual review.

policies.title: Development - Self-contained, no remote code execution
policies.fix: Bundle all scripts in the .xpi. Do not load and execute remote code.

policies.title: Development - Must not load/redirect to a remote new tab page
policies.fix: Host the new tab page inside the add-on, not on a remote URL.

policies.title: Development - Must not relax web page security headers (CSP)
policies.fix: Do not weaken page CSP. Remove unsafe-eval and unsafe-inline.

policies.title: Development - Use encryption for remote data transport
policies.fix: Use HTTPS for all data transport.

policies.title: Development - Avoid redundant code or files
policies.fix: Remove unused code, libraries, and assets from the package.

policies.title: Development - Must not negatively impact Firefox performance
policies.fix: Profile your add-on. Avoid CPU/memory regressions and synchronous heavy work.

policies.title: Development - Only release versions of third-party libraries, unmodified
policies.fix: Bundle official released library versions only; do not patch them in-place.

policies.title: Development - No obfuscated code
policies.fix: Mozilla rejects deliberately obfuscated code. Minification is allowed.

policies.title: User Scripts API - User script managers only
policies.fix: userScripts API is reserved for user-script-manager add-ons that let users install/manage scripts.

policies.title: Data - No search-term interception or transmission
policies.fix: Do not transmit search terms or intercept searches going to a third-party search provider.

policies.title: Data - No transmission of ancillary information
policies.fix: Only transmit data required for your stated functionality.

policies.title: Data - Browsing activity only as primary function
policies.fix: Transmission of browsing activity is only permitted as part of the stated primary function.

policies.title: Data Consent (Firefox >=140) - Use built-in classification taxonomy
policies.fix: If using Firefox's built-in data consent UI, declare your data classifications accurately in the manifest.

policies.title: Data Consent (Firefox <=139) - Unmissable in-add-on consent UI
policies.fix: Provide an unmissable, single-page consent UI immediately after install. No deceptive design patterns.

policies.title: Data Consent - Personal Data is opt-in
policies.fix: Personal data must be transmitted only after explicit user opt-in.

policies.title: Data Consent - Implicit consent for self-evident, single-use add-ons
policies.fix: Implicit consent only applies to single-use, self-evident add-ons that meet all five Mozilla conditions.

policies.title: Data Consent - Technical & interaction data is opt-out
policies.fix: Allow users to disable technical/interaction telemetry without losing core features.

policies.title: Privacy - No leaking local/user-specific info to web or native apps
policies.fix: Do not expose local data via DOM, postMessage, or native messaging.

policies.title: Privacy - Private browsing data must not be stored
policies.fix: Do not persist data captured in private browsing windows or expose cross-container identifiers.

policies.title: Monetization - Injected ads must be clearly identified
policies.fix: Any ad injected into web content must be clearly attributed to your add-on.

policies.title: Monetization - No cryptocurrency miners
policies.fix: Remove all cryptocurrency miners from the add-on.

policies.title: Monetization - No silent affiliate redirect/injection
policies.fix: Do not modify web content or redirects to insert affiliate tags. Affiliate links inside the add-on UI are OK.

policies.title: Reported rejection: minified/transpiled code without source-code submission
policies.fix: Submit original source plus build instructions via the AMO source-code submission form when you ship bundled/minified output.

policies.title: Reported rejection: third-party library shipped without upstream URL/version
policies.fix: For each bundled library, document name, version and origin URL in source-submission notes so reviewers can diff against upstream.

policies.title: Reported rejection: dynamic code execution (eval / new Function / setTimeout-string)
policies.fix: Replace eval/new Function with statically declared code. Pass functions, not strings, to setTimeout/setInterval.

policies.title: Reported rejection: loading remote scripts / WASM not bundled
policies.fix: Bundle every script, worker, and wasm file inside the .xpi. AMO blocks add-ons that fetch executable code at runtime.

policies.title: Reported rejection: changes search engine / homepage / new tab without explicit consent
policies.fix: Surface a clear opt-in dialog before overriding new-tab, homepage, or search defaults. Provide a one-click revert.

policies.title: Reported rejection: undisclosed telemetry / data collection
policies.fix: Disclose all data collection in the listing and obtain explicit user consent. Mozilla rejects silent analytics.

policies.title: Reported rejection: cross-origin XHR/fetch beyond declared host permissions
policies.fix: Match every fetch/XHR origin to a declared host permission and justify it in the AMO listing.
